opp.ais_why:
• Avoma 9-Feb-2026 strategic discussion shows AI is the buyer's lens with verbatim customer-stated needs: 'Automate end-to-end S2P processes with AI agents', 'Unify AI across modules to avoid fragmented quick wins', 'Integrate procurement capabilities directly within Microsoft Teams via AI chatbot'. Gabriel Hide (procurement lead) called the demo 'hyper interessant'. The decision routes via SVP Finance Feb 16 then March final, with AI agents central to the InTech strategy.
• Formal Evaluation stage, BD_Engagement Engaged. 2 Avoma meetings Feb 2026 (latest 9-Feb-2026, ~71 min) with Corentin Sebe and Gabriel Hide on the Ubisoft side. Products include Merlin and Merlin Intake plus iContract, iRequest, iRisk, iSupplier and eInvoicing ‚Äî full AI-bundled S2P. The 'Unify AI across modules' framing from the buyer is anti-fragmentation, pro-unified-AI-platform ‚Äî a perfect Zycus positioning fit.
• High confidence: direct buyer voice in Avoma frames AI as a transformation requirement, not just a feature. The 'unify AI across modules' line is buyer-spoken, not rep-spoken. Held below 9 because a formal RFP / mandated AI POC has not yet been declared. A signed POC scope or RFP with AI agent mandate would push to 9-10.

metrics.narrative: Ubisoft articulated operational pain around intake process inefficiency and desire for AI-centric procurement automation, but no quantified business case (cost savings, cycle time reduction, headcount impact, or ROI) has been stated. Next_Step__c entries (09 Apr, 04 Jun) note Ubisoft is 'International / Tech Company / AI centric' and RFP is 'Very Intake centric approach', signaling intake automation as the primary pain point. However, no buyer quote quantifies the current-state cost or target savings. Next_Step__c 21 May: 'RFP very intake oriented so on a pure solution point of view we should have a good ranking'. This suggests Ubisoft values intake capability but has not shared the financial or operational metrics that justify the investment. The absence of a quantified value case is a gap that must be closed before commercials. Recommend running an ROI workshop with the Economic Buyer to surface savings, cycle time, and compliance metrics.

decision_process.narrative: Ubisoft is running a formal, structured RFP process with defined milestones and a clear sequence. The decision process is mapped in the Next_Step__c RFP timeline table (04 Jun): (1) RFP issued 17 Apr, (2) Bidder questions due 30 Apr, (3) Offers due 15 May, (4) Evaluation by 05 Jun, (5) Demos with shortlisted vendors Jun, (6) Ubisoft feedback Jul. The process is procurement-led, highly structured, and intake-focused. Zycus was shortlisted (3-4 vendors) early Jun (Next_Step__c 01 Jun: 'Ubisoft we are shortlisted (3 or 4) they sent us even invitation prior to the short list being finalized'). Oral defense was 10 Jun (Next_Step__c 15 Jun). Next steps per Next_Step__c 24 Jun: technical meeting early Jul TBC, then if preferred, SoW workshop mid-Jul and short POC. Next_Step__c 24 Jun notes: 'Email received from Ubisoft mentioning that they still have to align internally before moving forward with a technical meeting on Integration / implementation. Frédéric Hamelin (DM direct report of EB Franz Pinto) confirmed via mail that it is really only an internal alignment we do not have to be worried'. This signals the process is on track but paused for internal alignment (not a Zycus issue). Approval chain: DM Anne-Sophie Dupont (sponsor, head of procurement excellence per Next_Step__c 27 Apr) and EB Franz Pinto. No indication of additional C-level or CFO approval required beyond Franz.

decision_criteria.narrative: Ubisoft's decision criteria are embedded in the formal RFP process and use-case evaluation structure. The RFP is 'Very Intake centric' (Next_Step__c 04 Jun), prioritizing intake automation capability above all. Ubisoft sent use cases on 03 Jun for the 10 Jun oral defense (Next_Step__c 01 Jun, 04 Jun: 'Uses cases received need to ensure we are spot on (they are aligned with their priorities)'). The oral defense on 10 Jun was use-case-aligned, and Next_Step__c 04 Jun notes: 'Presentation on 10th of June will be key'. Ubisoft's RFP timeline (Next_Step__c 04 Jun table) shows a structured evaluation: RFP issued 17 Apr, questions due 30 Apr, offers due 15 May, evaluation by 05 Jun, demos with shortlisted vendors Jun, feedback Jul. Criteria confirmed: (1) Intake automation depth (core use case), (2) AI/Merlin capability (Ubisoft is 'AI centric' per Next_Step__c 09 Apr), (3) integration capability (technical meeting early Jul TBC will validate PeopleSoft, Oracle ERP, Docusign, external compliance tool integration per prior discovery), (4) deployment model (centralized vs decentralized, offshore readiness per Next_Step__c 27 Apr call to Sofian). The technical meeting early Jul will surface any integration or deployment blockers that could disqualify Zycus.

Convert Frederic Hamelin from developing coach to active champion: he replied warmly on 24 Jun and reports to EB Franz Pinto, making him the best route to internal intel on the unnamed peers and to budget priority.

Beat the do-nothing/internal scenario, not just other vendors: arm the buyer with a quantified ROI/business case (~EUR 5M spend under management, 2-3% savings) to justify external investment despite Ubisoft's tight finances.

hard.ais_why:
• Avoma 9-Feb-2026 strategic discussion shows AI is the buyer's lens with verbatim customer-stated needs: 'Automate end-to-end S2P processes with AI agents', 'Unify AI across modules to avoid fragmented quick wins', 'Integrate procurement capabilities directly within Microsoft Teams via AI chatbot'. Gabriel Hide (procurement lead) called the demo 'hyper interessant'. The decision routes via SVP Finance Feb 16 then March final, with AI agents central to the InTech strategy.
• Formal Evaluation stage, BD_Engagement Engaged. 2 Avoma meetings Feb 2026 (latest 9-Feb-2026, ~71 min) with Corentin Sebe and Gabriel Hide on the Ubisoft side. Products include Merlin and Merlin Intake plus iContract, iRequest, iRisk, iSupplier and eInvoicing ‚Äî full AI-bundled S2P. The 'Unify AI across modules' framing from the buyer is anti-fragmentation, pro-unified-AI-platform ‚Äî a perfect Zycus positioning fit.
• High confidence: direct buyer voice in Avoma frames AI as a transformation requirement, not just a feature. The 'unify AI across modules' line is buyer-spoken, not rep-spoken. Held below 9 because a formal RFP / mandated AI POC has not yet been declared. A signed POC scope or RFP with AI agent mandate would push to 9-10.
